commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the QA-lab auth-profile staging helper to ignore only missing auth stores, validate existing profile containers, preserve supported credential shapes, and add focused Vitest coverage.

PR surface: Source +141, Tests +275. Total +416 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, though I did not execute it: current main catches any auth-profiles.json read or parse failure and then writes a replacement store during QA credential staging.
commands.review_followup: The protected maintainer label and auth-store compatibility risk need human review; there is no narrow automated repair to request from ClawSweeper.

target.title: fix(qa): preserve corrupt auth profile files
target.body:
## Summary
- make QA auth profile staging ignore only missing `auth-profiles.json` files instead of swallowing corrupt reads/parses
- validate existing profile containers before merging, while preserving shipped raw auth-profile shapes such as SecretRefs, OAuth sidecar refs, and legacy aliases
- add focused regression coverage for missing, corrupt, malformed, and compatibility-preserving auth stores

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/shared/auth-store.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-skills/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/shared/auth-store.test.ts"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: QA mock/live auth staging no longer replaces corrupt `auth-profiles.json` files with a new store when adding QA credentials.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, focused Vitest file for the QA auth profile store.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/shared/auth-store.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: tests assert invalid JSON and malformed profile containers reject while leaving the original file unchanged; compatibility tests preserve existing SecretRef, OAuth, legacy OAuth sidecar, and `mode`/`apiKey` alias profiles.
Observed result after fix: 1 test file passed, 7 tests passed.
What was not tested: full QA-lab suite and live provider staging were not run.
target.author: steipete
